JEE Main marks to rank shift

faq.q: What is a good score in JEE Main to get under 10,000 rank?
faq.a: Generally, a score of 180+ out of 300 is required to get a rank under 10,000. However, this varies depending on the exam difficulty level and year.

faq.q: How does category reservation affect JEE Main rank prediction?
faq.a: Reservation shifts the cutoff and category ranking. Rankr predicts your common merit list (CRL) rank, while category status determines the adjusted percentile needed for seat allocation.

faq.q: How accurate is the JEE Main rank predictor?
faq.a: Rankr uses linear interpolation based on massive historical datasets from 2023, 2024, and 2025, yielding up to 95%+ confidence when nearby data density is high.

faq.q: What rank is expected for 150 marks in JEE Main?
faq.a: Based on historical trends, 150 marks in JEE Main typically yields a Common Merit List (CRL) rank between 18,000 and 28,000, depending on the session difficulty.

faq.q: What NEET score is required for a government MBBS seat?
faq.a: For the General category, a NEET score of 610+ is typically needed for a government college MBBS seat through the All India Quota, though state quotas may have lower thresholds.

faq.q: Why do NEET marks-to-rank trends shift so much each year?
faq.a: NEET ranks fluctuate heavily based on the overall difficulty of the paper and the performance of top-scoring candidates, which is why historical comparisons are vital.

faq.q: What rank will I get for 600 marks in NEET?
faq.a: Historically, 600 marks in NEET corresponds to an All India Rank of approximately 20,000 to 30,000. However, in years with extreme score inflation (such as 2024), the rank can slide to 70,000+.

keywords: GATE category rank OBC SC ST

faq.q: What is a good rank in GATE for PSU recruitment?
faq.a: For top public sector undertakings (PSUs), a GATE rank within the top 200–500 in core branches is usually required for General category candidates.

faq.q: How does the GATE normalization process work?
faq.a: For multi-session papers, GATE normalizes marks using standard formulas based on the mean and standard deviation of top performers, which this predictor accounts for.

faq.q: What is a safe GATE score for IIT Bombay M.Tech?
faq.a: Generally, a normalized GATE score above 750 (often corresponding to a rank under 300) is considered safe for core engineering M.Tech branches at top-tier IITs.

keywords: CAT percentile calculator NC OBC SC ST

faq.q: What CAT percentile corresponds to a raw score of 100?
faq.a: Usually, a raw score of 95–100 out of 198 yields a 99+ percentile in CAT, putting you in the top tier for IIM calls.

faq.q: Does CAT rank determine IIM admissions?
faq.a: Yes, raw marks map to percentiles and ranks, which are then combined with academic profile (10th/12th/Graduation), work experience, and gender diversity points.

faq.q: What CAT percentile is required for IIM Ahmedabad?
faq.a: Generally, General category candidates require a CAT percentile of 99.5+ along with a stellar academic profile to get a call from IIM Ahmedabad.

faq.q: What is a good rank in WBJEE for Jadavpur University CSE?
faq.a: Generally, a GMR under 100 is required for Jadavpur University Computer Science & Engineering (CSE) for General category candidates, which typically corresponds to a score of 145+ out of 200 depending on the year's difficulty.

faq.q: What is the difference between GMR and PMR in WBJEE?
faq.a: GMR (General Merit Rank) is based on Paper 1 (Mathematics) and Paper 2 (Physics & Chemistry) combined, used for engineering admissions. PMR (Pharmacy Merit Rank) is based solely on Paper 2 and is used for pharmacy admissions.

faq.q: How does the negative marking system work in WBJEE?
faq.a: WBJEE has three categories of questions. Category I has -0.25 negative marking, Category II has -0.50 negative marking, and Category III has no negative marking, which makes fractional scores highly accurate.
